concepts.name: Linked List Basics
concepts.short_definition: Understanding nodes, data, and pointers in a linked list structure.

concepts.name: Node Structure
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental building block of a linked list, containing data and a reference to the next node.

concepts.name: Stack Operations
concepts.short_definition: The core functions of a stack: push, pop, peek, and isEmpty.

concepts.name: Push Operation
concepts.short_definition: Adding an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop Operation
concepts.short_definition: Removing and returning the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek Operation
concepts.short_definition: Returning the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: isEmpty Operation
concepts.short_definition: Determining if the stack contains any elements.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that always points to the first node (top) of the linked list representing the stack.

concepts.name: Dynamic Memory
concepts.short_definition: The ability of the linked list to grow or shrink as needed by allocating and deallocating memory.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the efficiency of stack operations when implemented using a linked list.
